title: Stable Merging Sort with Incorrect Comparison

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a stable merging sort algorithm. The algorithm should correctly handle duplicate elements within the array. For this bug fix challenge, the function should work correctly with the following time and space constraints: O(n log n) time complexity, and O(n) space complexity, where n is the number of elements in the array.
statement.inputFormat: The input is provided as an array of unique integers in the range [1, 1000) of size 'n' (1 <= n <= 10^4).
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a sorted array of integers in non-decreasing order.
statement.notes: The algorithm is a stable sorting algorithm, which means it maintains the relative order of equal elements in the original array.

examples.explanation: The input array has 4 elements. The correct output is the sorted array in non-decreasing order.

hints.text: First hint text: Check the merge function's logic for handling equal elements.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Stable Merging Sort
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Implement the merge function to compare elements correctly.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int* mergeSort(int* nums, int n) { if (n == 1) return nums; if (nums[0] < nums[n-1]) { int* temp = new int[n]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) temp[i] = nums[i]; delete[] nums; nums = temp; } return mergeSort(nums+1, n-1); } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int[] mergeSort(int[] nums, int n) { if (n == 1) return nums; if (nums[0] < nums[n-1]) { int[] temp = new int[n]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) temp[i] = nums[i]; nums = temp; } return mergeSort(Arrays.copyOfRange(nums, 1, n), n-1); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def mergeSort(self, nums: list[int]) -> list[int]: if len(nums) == 1: return nums; if nums[0] < nums[-1]: # incorrect comparison nums = [nums[0]] + nums[1:-1] + [nums[-1]]; return mergeSort(nums[1:-1]);
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function mergeSort(nums) { if (nums.length == 1) return nums; if (nums[0] < nums[nums.length-1]) { nums = [nums[0]] + nums.slice(1, -1) + [nums[nums.length-1]]; return mergeSort(nums); } }
